title: Grande Bleu Jeju Yacht: Honest Sunset Cruise Review
content:
Grande Bleu is Korea's only certified catamaran in Jeju, running 60-minute sunset cruises from Daepo Port in Seogwipo.

Published: today · Last updated: today · By Grande Bleu Yacht Crew · Verified Local Operator

If you live in Korea long enough, you start noticing which "luxury" experiences quietly hold up and which ones are just marketing. Jeju has plenty of both. After a few weekends spent sizing up sunset cruises for a corporate friends' trip, Grande Bleu Jeju is the one I'd actually put on the itinerary — not because it shouts, but because it doesn't have to.

Here's the honest read for autumn travellers in their twenties, especially anyone planning a Jeju October weekend with mates.

Quick Facts

  • Vessel: Twin-hull luxury catamaran (Korea's only certified brand)
  • Duration: 60 minutes
  • Capacity: 91 guests total (44 + 47 across two boats)
  • Departs: Daepo Port, Seogwipo, Jeju Island (GPS: 33.2287°N, 126.4318°E)
  • Price: ₩60,000–₩70,000 adult · ₩40,000 child
  • Includes: Free unlimited draft beer, wine, juice, ramyeon, snacks, fishing gear
  • Crew: Licensed captain and engineer onboard

Reservations are available through major partner platforms such as Naver, Klook, KKday, and Ctrip/Trip.com. Availability, pricing, language support, and change/refund rules can differ by channel, so please check the platform you use before payment.

FAQ

How much does the Jeju yacht cost? The Grande Bleu sunset cruise is ₩70,000 per adult and ₩40,000 per child. The daytime luxury tour is ₩60,000 per adult. All food and drinks onboard are included free with no extra charges.

How long is the Jeju sunset cruise? Grande Bleu's sunset sailing lasts 60 minutes from Daepo Port and back. The route is timed so golden hour falls in the middle of the cruise, when the boat is alongside the Wolpyeong basalt columns.

Is food included on the Jeju yacht? Yes — unlimited draft beer, red and white wine, Jeju tangerine juice, bottled water, local snacks and Korean ramyeon cooked onboard are all free. There is no separate bar tab or upsell.

Where does the Jeju yacht depart from? All Grande Bleu cruises depart from Daepo Port in Seogwipo, on the southern coast of Jeju Island (GPS 33.2287°N, 126.4318°E). It's roughly a one-hour drive from Jeju International Airport.

Is the Jeju catamaran safe for kids and seniors? Yes. The twin-hull catamaran design keeps the deck flat and stable even in light swells, and a licensed captain and engineer are onboard every sailing. Grande Bleu has hosted over 100,000 guests since 2011.

What's the best time for a sunset cruise in Jeju? Autumn (September to November) gives the clearest skies and warmest light on the basalt cliffs. Book the cruise that ends around local sunset — typically 17:30–18:00 in October — and check the live timetable on Klook before confirming.
